label: rest980 MCP
info:
MCP tools for iRobot Roomba via rest980 (https://github.com/koalazak/rest980).
Set env var REST980_URL to the rest980 base URL, e.g. http://192.168.1.50:3000
Set mcpServer on each MCPIn node to your event handler node ID.

name: iRobot status
description: Returns the current status of the iRobot Roomba: cleaning cycle (none/clean/quick/spot/evac/dock/train), phase (charge/run/stuck/stop/hmPostMsn/hmUsrDock/dockend/idle), battery percentage, cleaning time, area covered in sqft, error code, and pmap_id (map ID needed for irobot_clean_room). Call this to check if the robot is running, docked, or stuck.

name: Build request
Format response
iRobot action
description: Send a command to the iRobot Roomba. Use 'start' to begin cleaning, 'stop' to halt, 'pause' to pause, 'resume' to continue after pause, or 'dock' to send the robot back to its charging dock.

name: Validate + build request
Format response
iRobot clean room
description: Start a targeted room cleaning session. Requires the pmap_id (map ID) and region_ids (room IDs) — get pmap_id from irobot_status. The robot will clean only the specified rooms. Use ordered=true (default) to clean in sequence.
inputSchema: {"type":"object","required":["pmap_id","region_ids"],"properties":{"pmap_id":{"type":"string","description":"Map ID — get from irobot_status pmap_id field"},"region_ids":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"Room IDs to clean"},"user_pmapv_id":{"type":"string","description":"Map version string (optional)"},"ordered":{"type":"boolean","description":"Clean rooms in order (default: true)"}}}

name: Build request
Format response
